ground_truth.title: WHITE HOUSE DENIES NIXON'S CUBA CHARGE

cab.declassify_on: NA to NATO portions, see source list for NSI portions

paragraphs.text: White House Denies Nixon's Cuba Charge
By E. W. KENWORTHY Special to The New York Times.

paragraphs.mark: //JOINT TS AUS CAN ITA USA//REL TO USA, AUS, CAN, ITA, EU
paragraphs.text: WASHINGTON, March 20 The White House denied today a charge by Richard M. Nixon that President Kennedy had been informed of the secret preparations for a refugee landing in Cuba before he issued his 1960 campaign call for United States aid to anti-Castro forces.

Allen W. Dulles, who was head of the Central Intelligence Agency at the time, supported the President's reconstruction of events, but said he believed Mr. Nixon's statement to be the result of "an honest misunderstanding."
In a book entitled "Six Crises," scheduled for publication March 29, the former Vice President refers to a speech on Oct. 20, 1960, in which Mr. Kennedy advocated support for those
